name: California Style Huevos Rancheros
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Tomato juice, OR 1/2 cup tomato sauce thinned with 2 tbsp water
3 Whole chiles, parched, peeled and chopped, OR 4 oz canned whole green chiles, chopped
2 Avocados, peeled and thinly sliced lengthwise
1/2 cup Grated Monterey Jack or Cheddar cheese
recipeInstructions:
...Serve fresh fruit, warm wheat-flour tortillas, sweet butter, honey, and coffee for a great brunch.
1. In a medium-size saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and fry until translucent. Stir in tomato juice or tomato sauce thinned with water, garlic, chiles, herbs, and salt. Reduce heat to simmer for about 10 minutes. The sauce can be made up to 24 hours ahead and reheated.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F. Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in foil and placing on serving plates (Mexican pottery if possible) in the warm oven. Also, warm extra tortillas as bread.
3. Poach the eggs using an egg poacher or a frying pan of hot water with 2 tablespoons vinegar and 1 teaspoon salt added. Break each egg into a cup, then stir the water in the pan vigorously in a circular motion. Slide the egg from the cup into the water when all eggs are added.
4. To assemble the dish, place a tortilla on a plate. Top with avocado slices, then arrange the poached eggs (or 2 eggs) on top, spoon sauce over, and sprinkle cheese. Place in the oven until cheese melts. Add lettuce garnish after the cheese has melted.
Variation: Instead of tortillas, rinse large "cupped" iceberg lettuce leaves and serve eggs topped with sauce in the lettuce leaves.
Makes 4 servings.
From: JANE BUTEL'S TEX-MEX COOKBOOK by Jane Butel, Harmony Books, New York. 1980. ISBN 0-517-539861 Shared by: Karin Brewer, Cooking Echo, 6/93
